echo (Energy and Commodity Holistic Optimiser)
  • »
  • echo Port object
  • View page source

echo Port object

Ports terminate a network edge and represent the connection of an edge to a node. Ports are associated with an individual commodity and represent the flow of the that commodity into or out of a node.

Port variables

All ports have at least one variable, \(p_{x, t}\) the port flow value.

If the port needs to be split into a positive and negative component, we introduce the additional variables:

\(p_{x, t}^+\), the positive component of the port flow

\(p_{x, t}^-\), the negative component of the port flow

\(p_{x, t}^{*}\), a binary variable for indicating whether \(p^+\) is non-zero.

The following three constraints are used to split the variable.

\(p_{x, t} &= p^+_{x, t} + p^-_{x, t}\)

\(p^+_{x, t} &\leq p^*_{x, t} \cdot M\)

\(p^-_{x, t} &\geq (p^*_{x, t} - 1) \cdot M\)

Port validation

What is intended to go here?
